sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Alexandria Real Estate Equities Begins $800 Million Tender Offer From Pasadena Headquarters
feed.summary: Alexandria Real Estate Equities, headquartered in Pasadena, has initiated cash tender offers to repurchase up to $800 million of its outstanding senior notes. The offer targets specific series of senior notes due in 2050, 2051, and 2052, with an early tender premium for notes submitted by February 9th. The company's obligation to complete the tender offers is contingent on securing at least $500 million in gross proceeds from other capital markets financing transactions.

feed.title: CSX Corporation honors Catherine Adkins for pioneering real estate innovation
feed.summary: CSX Corporation has recognized Catherine Adkins, their Director of Real Estate, for her innovative contributions to modernizing real estate systems and advancing AI technologies over two decades. Adkins, also named to the 2026 Women of Influence in Rail, has been instrumental in positioning CSX as a forward-thinking transportation industry leader. This recognition highlights CSX's commitment to technology and talent, aligning with broader initiatives to enhance operational efficiency and strategic growth within the rail sector.

feed.title: Stock Traders Buy Large Volume of Put Options on Keurig Dr Pepper (NASDAQ:KDP)
feed.summary: Traders significantly increased their bearish bets on Keurig Dr Pepper (NASDAQ:KDP), buying 43,191 put options in one day, a 225% jump over average daily volume. This occurred as the stock price fell to $26.71, trading below its 50- and 200-day moving averages, despite a "Moderate Buy" consensus from analysts. Insider activity also showed a VP selling 10,000 shares, while institutional investors like Vanguard and Norges Bank hold a significant majority of the shares.

feed.title: New Trio of AAM ETFs Use Different Growth Strategies
feed.summary: Advisors Asset Management (AAM) has launched three new active ETFs on NYSE Arca: the AAM Brentview Dividend Growth ETF (BDIV), the AAM Sawgrass U.S. Small Cap Quality Growth ETF (SAWS), and the AAM Sawgrass U.S. Large Cap Quality Growth ETF (SAWG). BDIV focuses on dividend growth with lower beta, while SAWS and SAWG employ proprietary modeling for quality growth in small and large-cap segments, respectively. These additions bring AAM's total ETFs to eight, with over $460 million in assets under management.

feed.title: Las Vegas Sands' quarterly profit rises on strong Macau demand
feed.summary: Las Vegas Sands reported a significant rise in its fourth-quarter profit, driven by strong demand from its Macau operations. The company's total revenue increased by 26% year-over-year to $3.6 billion, surpassing analyst expectations. CEO Robert Goldstein attributed this growth to long-standing investments aimed at boosting Macau's business and leisure tourism.

feed.title: Nike vs. Lululemon: Which Stock to Buy for 2026?
feed.summary: Both Nike (NKE) and Lululemon (LULU) stocks are significantly down from their highs, struggling in a challenging consumer environment. While Nike's larger size and diverse operations are slowing its recovery, Lululemon is positioned for a quicker turnaround due to faster revenue growth and a healthy inventory position. Lululemon also appears to be a better value, trading at a lower forward price-to-earnings multiple compared to Nike.

feed.title: First Patient Dosed in Pivotal Phase 3 Study of TLX591-Px (Illuccix®) for Prostate Cancer Imaging in Japanese Patients
feed.summary: Telix Pharmaceuticals Limited announced that the first patient has been dosed in a pivotal Phase 3 study for TLX591-Px (Illuccix®) in Japanese patients with prostate cancer. This multi-center study will evaluate the detection efficacy and safety of 68 Ga-PSMA-11 PET/CT imaging for biochemically recurrent prostate cancer, aiming to support a future marketing authorization in Japan. The trial is a significant step towards improving access to advanced diagnostic imaging for prostate cancer in Japan, where incidence rates are increasing.

feed.title: Tesh Thomas, chief admin. officer, sells $56k in Rollins (ROL)
feed.summary: Rollins Chief Administrative Officer Tesh Thomas D. sold 887 shares of common stock for approximately $56,111 to cover tax withholding obligations related to restricted stock vesting. This transaction occurred with ROL near its 52-week high and a 29% one-year return. Despite Thomas's remaining substantial holdings, InvestingPro suggests Rollins is overvalued with a high P/E ratio, though it notes a history of consistent dividend payments.

feed.title: Rollins CLO Chandler sells $136k in company stock By Investing.com
feed.summary: Elizabeth B. Chandler, Chief Legal Officer of Rollins Inc, sold 2,151 shares of company stock worth $136,072 to cover tax withholding obligations. After this transaction, Chandler directly owns 60,161 shares. The article also notes recent analyst ratings and a declared quarterly cash dividend for Rollins Inc.

feed.title: Rollins executive chairman emeritus sells $1.2m in stock By Investing.com
feed.summary: Rollins Executive Chairman Emeritus Gary W. Rollins sold 19,094 shares of common stock for over $1.2 million, primarily to cover tax withholding obligations related to restricted stock vesting. The pest control company, with a market cap of $30.17 billion, has a strong track record of dividend payments and share performance. Recent analyst ratings from UBS, RBC Capital, and Morgan Stanley show varied price targets and ratings, reflecting different perspectives on Rollins' valuation and growth potential.

feed.title: Musk Says Tesla Needs to Build ‘TeraFab’ to Manufacture Chips
feed.summary: Elon Musk announced that Tesla plans to build a "TeraFab" for manufacturing semiconductors, a move he says is necessary to avert a probable constraint in chip supply within three to four years. This large-scale facility will incorporate logic, memory, and packaging capabilities and signifies a significant expansion for Tesla beyond its traditional electric vehicle manufacturing. The project is expected to cost billions of dollars and will operate domestically.

feed.title: Tractor Supply rival chain shutting all locations soon
feed.summary: Cascade Farm and Outdoor, a rival to Tractor Supply that serves rural communities, is closing all of its stores in the spring. The company, a division of Bi-Mart, stated that while the Cascade stores were successful, they lacked the volume and momentum to significantly contribute to Bi-Mart's future path. This closure will leave a void in the Pacific Northwest's rural areas, similar to how other retail closures have impacted communities with limited shopping options.

feed.title: Adtalem (NYSE:ATGE) Posts Better-Than-Expected Sales In Q4 CY2025
feed.summary: Adtalem Global Education (NYSE:ATGE) reported better-than-expected Q4 CY2025 results, with sales up 12.4% year-on-year to $503.4 million, exceeding market estimates. The company's non-GAAP profit of $2.43 per share was also above analysts' consensus, and management raised its full-year Adjusted EPS guidance. Despite strong revenue and EPS beats, adjusted EBITDA missed expectations.

feed.title: Earnings call transcript: Lam Research Q2 2026 sees earnings beat, stock rises
feed.summary: Lam Research (LRCX) exceeded Q2 2026 earnings and revenue forecasts, reporting an EPS of $1.27 against an anticipated $1.16, and revenue of $5.34 billion over expected $5.21 billion. The company's stock rose 1.08% in after-hours trading. Lam Research also achieved record annual revenue and gross margin for 2025, driven by strong demand in semiconductor markets and the AI transformation, and anticipates continued growth in 2026.

feed.title: Earnings call transcript: Raymond James Financial Q1 2026 beats EPS estimates
feed.summary: Raymond James Financial reported its Q1 2026 earnings, exceeding EPS expectations with an adjusted $2.86 per share, though revenue of $3.7 billion fell short of forecasts. The company's shares rose 0.58% in aftermarket trading. Key takeaways include strong performance in securities-based lending and the introduction of an AI-powered digital operations agent, "Rai," reflecting a commitment to technology and long-term growth.

feed.title: Netstreit reports 2025 tax treatment of common stock distributions By Investing.com
feed.summary: Netstreit Corp. (NYSE: NTST) announced the 2025 tax treatment for its common stock distributions, classifying them as a mix of ordinary dividends and non-dividend distributions. The company issued four quarterly distributions in 2025, with 86.3772% of each classified as ordinary dividends and the remaining as non-dividend distributions, with no portion identified as capital gains or qualified dividends. All ordinary dividends qualified under Section 199A for 2025, reflecting the company's dividend growth and strong financial health despite a recent third-quarter earnings miss.

feed.title: Meta Platforms Inc-Class A (NASDAQ:META) Beats Q4 2025 Estimates and Issues Strong Q1 Guidance Amid Major AI Spending Plans
feed.summary: Meta Platforms Inc-Class A (NASDAQ:META) reported strong Q4 2025 results, surpassing analyst estimates for both revenue and EPS, driven by its core advertising business. The company also issued optimistic Q1 2026 guidance, projecting higher revenue than consensus. Despite these strong financial performances, a significant highlight is Meta's aggressive investment strategy for 2026, focusing heavily on AI development and infrastructure, which will drive substantial capital outlays.

feed.title: ROSEN, HIGHLY RECOGNIZED INVESTOR COUNSEL, Encourages F5, Inc. Investors to Secure Counsel ...
feed.summary: Rosen Law Firm is encouraging investors of F5, Inc. (NASDAQ: FFIV) who purchased securities between October 28, 2024, and October 27, 2025, to secure legal counsel as they face a lead plaintiff deadline of February 17, 2026. The lawsuit alleges that F5, Inc. made false impressions regarding its projected revenue growth and downplayed risks, leading to investor damages when the true details came to light. The firm urges investors to choose qualified counsel with a strong track record in securities class action litigation.

feed.title: Waabi raises $750M as Uber separately commits up to $250M for robotaxi partnership
feed.summary: Canadian autonomous truck startup Waabi Innovation Inc. has secured $750 million in new funding, with a separate commitment of up to $250 million from Uber Technologies Inc. The capital infusion will fuel the expansion of Waabi’s Physical AI Platform for autonomous trucking and its entry into the robotaxi market. Uber's investment supports an exclusive partnership for deploying robotaxis powered by Waabi Driver on the Uber platform, aiming for 25,000 or more such vehicles over time.

feed.title: Earnings call transcript: Fair Isaac beats Q1 2026 forecasts, stock dips
feed.summary: Fair Isaac Corporation (FICO) reported strong Q1 2026 results, exceeding EPS and revenue forecasts with an EPS of $7.33 and revenue of $512 million. Despite this outperformance, the stock dipped 1.18% in aftermarket trading. The company maintained its fiscal 2026 guidance, highlighting growth in mortgage and auto originations, new partnerships, and continued investment in its software platform.

feed.title: Uber Technologies (NYSE:UBER) Shares Down 1.8% - What's Next?
feed.summary: Uber Technologies (UBER) shares dipped 1.8% to $79.77 on Wednesday, with trading volume slightly above average. The company has a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating from analysts with an average target price of $107.50, but faces legal and reputational risks, while insiders have recently reduced their positions. Uber is also making strategic moves in autonomous vehicle technology, investing in Waabi and launching an "AV Labs" division to accelerate robotaxi development.

feed.title: CalciMedica Discontinues Trial, Stock Faces Volatility
feed.summary: CalciMedica announced the discontinuation of its Auxora trial for acute kidney injury patients due to safety concerns, leading to a significant drop in its stock price. Despite the setback, analysts like Oppenheimer view the stock as undervalued, pointing to the acute pancreatitis opportunity. Retail investor sentiment on Stocktwits has shifted to "extremely bullish," indicating hopes for a rebound.

feed.title: Waste Management (NYSE:WM) Releases Earnings Results, Misses Expectations By $0.02 EPS
feed.summary: Waste Management (NYSE:WM) reported its quarterly earnings, announcing an EPS of $1.93, which missed the consensus estimate of $1.95 by $0.02. Despite this, the company maintains a "Moderate Buy" rating from analysts with an average target price of $251.19. The stock traded slightly up at $231.40, and the company also declared a quarterly dividend of $0.825 per share.

feed.title: Amprius Technologies (NYSE:AMPX) Trading 3.6% Higher - Here's Why
feed.summary: Amprius Technologies (NYSE:AMPX) saw its shares trade up 3.6% on Wednesday, reaching approximately $11.99, although insider selling has been significant with 3.36 million shares sold recently. Analysts have a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating with an average price target of $16, and the company reported better-than-expected earnings for the last quarter. Institutional investors have also been adjusting their positions in the stock.

feed.title: United Rentals Inc (NYSE:URI) Shares Fall on Q4 Revenue and Profit Miss
feed.summary: United Rentals Inc (NYSE:URI) reported record annual revenue for 2025 but missed Wall Street's Q4 revenue and profit estimates, leading to a sharp decline in its stock price. Despite the quarterly miss and margin compression, the company announced robust full-year results, significant capital returns to shareholders in 2025, and an optimistic 2026 outlook with a new $5 billion share repurchase program and a 10% dividend increase. The market reacted negatively to the short-term misses, but the company's strong balance sheet and commitment to shareholder returns aim to support investor sentiment.

feed.title: Earnings call transcript: Adtalem Global Education beats expectations in Q2 2026
feed.summary: Adtalem Global Education reported strong Q2 2026 results, exceeding revenue and adjusted EPS expectations with $503.4 million and $2.43 respectively, driven by enrollment growth and healthcare education focus. Despite the positive financial performance, the stock experienced a slight decline in after-hours trading. The company raised its full-year adjusted EPS guidance, demonstrating confidence in its strategic initiatives and market position as a leading healthcare educator.

feed.title: CalciMedica discontinues Phase 2 KOURAGE trial following committee recommendation
feed.summary: CalciMedica (NASDAQ:CALC) has announced the discontinuation of its Phase 2 KOURAGE clinical trial for acute kidney injury, following a recommendation from its Independent Data Monitoring Committee due to safety concerns. This decision has led the company to update its financial guidance, now projecting that its current cash reserves will fund operations into the fourth quarter of 2026, extending its previous forecast. Despite the halted trial, Oppenheimer has maintained an Outperform rating and a $20.00 price target for CalciMedica, citing the significant unmet need for treating acute kidney injury.

feed.title: C.H. ROBINSON WORLDWIDE INC (NASDAQ:CHRW) Beats Profit Expectations Amid Tough Freight Market
feed.summary: C.H. ROBINSON WORLDWIDE INC (CHRW) exceeded profit expectations in Q4 2025 despite a challenging freight market, leading to a significant stock price increase. The company's adjusted EPS of $1.23 surpassed estimates, driven by cost management, productivity improvements through its "Lean AI" strategy, and market share gains in North American Surface Transportation. While revenue declined, investors reacted positively to the strong bottom-line performance and commitment to shareholder returns.

feed.title: Home Depot to require employees work in office 5 days per week, cut 800 jobs: reports
feed.summary: Home Depot is reportedly mandating a full return to the office for employees, requiring them to work in person five days a week. Alongside this policy change, the company is also implementing job cuts, eliminating 800 positions in Atlanta. These decisions come as the home improvement retailer adjusts its operational strategies.

feed.title: Las Vegas Sands (LVS) Surpasses Earnings Expectations, Shares Di
feed.summary: Las Vegas Sands (LVS) reported strong Q4 results, with revenue increasing by 26.9% to $3.65 billion and EPS reaching $0.85, both surpassing analyst expectations. Despite this positive financial performance, the company's shares experienced a 6.4% decline in after-hours trading. The casino operator, which now generates all its EBITDA from Asia, exhibits robust financial health with strong margins but also faces risks associated with high leverage and market volatility.

feed.title: Ball Corporation declares 20 cents per share dividend
feed.summary: Ball Corporation has announced a quarterly cash dividend of 20 cents per share, continuing its 53-year streak of dividend payments. The company also scheduled its Q4 2025 earnings release for February 3, 2026, and has recently made strategic moves including acquiring an 80% stake in Benepack's European operations, refinancing credit facilities, and appointing a new CEO.

feed.title: Veritone's Impressive Performance in 2025
feed.summary: Veritone (VERI) saw impressive growth in 2025, with its shares soaring by nearly 42% due to strong demand for AI stocks and market confidence. The company secured a $10 million direct stock offering, a significant contract with the U.S. Air Force, and achieved over 200% year-over-year growth in non-legacy software revenue, boosting overall revenue by 32% to over $29 million. Analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating with an average 1-year price target of $8.33, reflecting optimism about its AI platform and market penetration.

feed.title: MSCI Inc (MSCI) Q4 2025 Earnings Call Highlights: Strong Revenue Growth and Strategic AI Integration
feed.summary: MSCI Inc. (MSCI) reported strong Q4 2025 earnings, with organic revenue growth exceeding 10%, adjusted EBITDA growth over 13%, and adjusted EPS growth of almost 12%. The company highlighted its long-term integration of AI in operations, ESG ratings, and product development, which is expected to enhance client services and insights. Despite some softness in sustainability product sales due to political factors in the US and anticipated higher cash taxes in 2026, MSCI expressed confidence in continued growth, particularly in private capital solutions and international markets.

feed.title: Avnet Inc (AVT) Q2 2026 Earnings Call Highlights: Strong Sales Growth and Positive Outlook Amid ...
feed.summary: Avnet Inc (AVT) reported strong Q2 2026 earnings, exceeding guidance with sales of $6.3 billion, a 12% year-over-year increase. The company generated over $200 million in cash flow from operations and saw robust book-to-bills across all regions, indicating strong demand. Despite positive results, Avnet faces challenges including supply chain mismatches, selective spot price increases for certain commodities, increased operating expenses, and difficulty in reducing inventory levels in specific areas.

feed.title: CONMED (NYSE:CNMD) Beats Q4 CY2025 Sales Expectations, Stock Soars
feed.summary: CONMED (NYSE:CNMD) reported Q4 CY2025 results that exceeded revenue expectations, with sales up 7.9% year-on-year to $373.2 million and non-GAAP EPS of $1.43, beating analyst estimates by 8%. However, the company's full-year revenue guidance for 2026 slightly missed analysts' estimates, and its operating margin decreased by 5.4 percentage points year-on-year in Q4. Despite mixed results, the stock traded up 5.4% immediately following the announcement, indicating a positive market reception to the revenue beat.

feed.title: (CNMD) CONMED Expects 2026 Revenue Range $1.35B-$1.38B, vs. FactSet Est of $1.38B
feed.summary: CONMED Corporation (CNMD) has provided its preliminary outlook for fiscal year 2026, anticipating total reported revenue to be in the range of $1.35 billion to $1.38 billion. This forecast is slightly below the FactSet estimate of $1.38 billion. The announcement was made following the company's presentation at the 44th Annual J.P. Morgan Healthcare Conference.

feed.title: CareTrust REIT Sets Fourth Quarter Earnings Call for Friday, February 13, 2026
feed.summary: CareTrust REIT, Inc. announced that it will release its fourth-quarter and full-year 2025 financial results after market close on Thursday, February 12, 2026. The company will host a conference call on Friday, February 13, 2026, at 1:00 p.m. Eastern Time to discuss these results and other current matters. A live audio webcast will be available on the Investors section of CareTrust's website, with a replay accessible shortly after.

feed.title: Ardelyx Draws Zydus Interest As IBSRELA Trial And Valuation Gain Focus
feed.summary: Ardelyx is catching investor attention due to reported interest from Zydus Lifesciences in acquiring a majority stake and the initiation of a Phase 3 clinical trial for its drug IBSRELA for chronic idiopathic constipation. The stock has shown strong recent performance, with returns of 37.5% over the past 30 days and 169.3% over three years, while still trading significantly below analyst targets and an estimated fair value. Investors are advised to monitor progress on the potential acquisition talks and the clinical trial, as these events could lead to further sharp movements in the share price.

feed.title: United Rentals (NYSE:URI) Misses Q4 CY2025 Sales Expectations, Stock Drops
feed.summary: United Rentals (NYSE:URI) missed Wall Street's revenue expectations for Q4 CY2025, with sales growing 2.8% to $4.21 billion, slightly below estimates. The company's non-GAAP profit also fell short of consensus estimates. Despite long-term revenue and EPS growth, recent performance shows a slowdown, leading to a stock drop immediately after the earnings report.

feed.title: Assurant appoints Lynn Blake to board of directors
feed.summary: Assurant (AIZ) has appointed Lynn Blake, a veteran asset manager formerly with State Street Investment Management, to its Board of Directors, effective January 28, 2026. Blake's extensive experience in asset management, including systematic equity strategies and sustainable investing, is expected to enhance Assurant’s financial strategy and risk oversight. Concurrently, director Debra Perry will retire from the board at the 2026 annual shareholder meeting as part of a planned board refresh.

feed.title: Axcelis Technologies Announces Merger with Veeco Instruments
feed.summary: Axcelis Technologies (ACLS) announced a merger agreement with Veeco Instruments on January 28, 2026, where Veeco will become a wholly-owned subsidiary. A special stockholder meeting is scheduled for February 6, 2026, though stockholder actions are challenging the merger disclosures. Axcelis has voluntarily supplemented its proxy statement to prevent litigation delays related to these challenges.

feed.title: L3Harris Technologies awarded $62.7 million contract modification By Investing.com
feed.summary: L3Harris Technologies Inc. has secured a $62.7 million modification to an existing contract from the U.S. Department of War, increasing the total contract value to $1.51 billion. This modification, linked to the Eglin Radar and Space Surveillance Telescope projects, is for Option Year Seven. Work will be performed in Colorado Springs, Colorado, with completion expected by January 31, 2027, and will be funded incrementally with fiscal 2026 operations, maintenance, research, development, test, and evaluation funds.

feed.title: Home Depot to cut 800 corporate jobs, require workers back to office full time
feed.summary: Home Depot is eliminating approximately 800 corporate jobs at its Vinings headquarters and will require corporate employees to return to the office five days a week starting April 6. CEO Ted Decker stated these changes are designed to increase the company's "speed and agility." This move follows similar actions by other large companies like UPS and Amazon in response to economic uncertainties, a slowing housing market, and a shift in labor market dynamics.

feed.title: Fair Isaac Corporation (NYSE:FICO) Posts Better-Than-Expected Sales In Q4 CY2025
feed.summary: Fair Isaac Corporation (FICO) exceeded Wall Street's revenue and adjusted EPS expectations in Q4 CY2025, with sales up 16.4% year-on-year to $512 million and EPS at $7.33. However, its full-year revenue guidance of $2.35 billion fell slightly below analysts' estimates. The company demonstrated strong long-term revenue and EPS growth, alongside improved operating margins and a reduced share count.

feed.title: Assurant Appoints Lynn Blake to Board of Directors – Company Announcement
feed.summary: Assurant, Inc. has appointed Lynn Blake, a former Executive Vice President and Chief Investment Officer at State Street Investment Management, to its Board of Directors, effective January 28, 2026. Blake brings over 30 years of experience in asset management, investment strategies, and risk management, and will serve on the company’s Finance and Risk Committee and Nominating and Corporate Governance Committee. Concurrently, Debra Perry plans to retire from the Board at Assurant's 2026 annual shareholder meeting.

feed.title: Assurant Appoints Lynn Blake to Board of Directors
feed.summary: Assurant, Inc. announced the appointment of Lynn Blake, former Executive Vice President and Chief Investment Officer at State Street Investment Management, to its Board of Directors, effective January 28, 2026. Blake brings over 30 years of experience in asset management, investment strategies, risk management, and corporate governance. She will serve on the company’s Finance and Risk Committee and Nominating and Corporate Governance Committee, contributing to Assurant's strategic priorities and financial strategy, while Debra Perry plans to retire from the Board in 2026.

feed.title: McDonald's brings back popular Changeables Happy Meal from the '80s
feed.summary: McDonald's has reintroduced its iconic Changeables Happy Meal, a fan favorite from the 1980s. These toys, similar to Transformers, switch between McDonald's food items and robot characters, and 16 different designs are available for a limited time. The launch aims to evoke nostalgia for both adults and children, with each toy featuring a unique trading card, fun fact, and catchphrase.

feed.title: Waste Management stock slips after Q4 earnings miss (WM:NYSE)
feed.summary: Waste Management (WM) shares dropped 2% in extended trading after the company reported fourth-quarter earnings below Wall Street expectations, with adjusted earnings of $1.93 a share against an anticipated $1.95. Despite this, the company projects increased revenue, EBITDA, and free cash flow for 2026, planning to return approximately $3.5 billion to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases. Improved operating margins and cash flow in the past quarter were attributed to cost controls, operational efficiency, and investments in recycling automation and renewable natural gas.

feed.title: Axos Financial Q2 2026 earnings preview
feed.summary: This article provides an earnings preview for Axos Financial's Q2 2026 results. It focuses on the upcoming financial report for the company.

feed.title: C.H. Robinson Worldwide (NASDAQ:CHRW) Releases Quarterly Earnings Results, Beats Expectations By $0.12 EPS
feed.summary: C.H. Robinson Worldwide (NASDAQ:CHRW) reported strong quarterly earnings, beating analyst expectations by $0.12 with an EPS of $1.23, although revenue slightly missed targets at $3.91 billion. The stock saw a significant jump, trading at $184.47, near its 12-month high, and the company raised its quarterly dividend. Analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating with several firms increasing price targets.
